market_summary: REGIME: Mixed/Cautious with defensive rotation intact. SPY -0.89% 1m, QQQ -3.47% 1m and below MA50 — tech leadership continues to erode. IWM +0.48% 1m / +11.25% 3m outperforming large-cap, signaling rotation toward cyclicals and small/mid. TLT +3.21% 1m confirms bond bid as rates expectations shift. Gold (GLD mom=+39.16%, 3m=+25.59%) remains dominant safe-haven and momentum leader. Risk-on breadth fragmenting: energy and commodities leading while mega-cap tech and growth rolls over.

LEADERSHIP: Energy (XOM mom=+28.38%, CVX mom=+19.18%) and Commodities (GLD mom=+39.16%) hold top portfolio positions with clean momentum. KLAC (mom=+53.17%) remains standout semiconductor quality name, well above MA50/MA200. Industrials (XLI mom=+18.56%, dd63=-2.66%) are the lowest-risk momentum holding. LMT (mom=+35.36%, 3m=+43.33%, dd63=-5.23%) continues to build case as highest-conviction defense add candidate for next rebalance. TSM (mom=+51.14%, 3m=+32.74%, 1m=+11.37%) is the strongest AVGO replacement candidate. AVGO (3m=-14.72%, dd63=-25.26%) remains the portfolio's weakest link with deteriorating momentum.

NO-GO: Rebalance due today is FALSE. Day 1 of minimum 7-day hold cadence following Feb-26 run. No emergency conditions triggered — all 8 holdings remain above invalidation thresholds (AVGO above $300 kill-switch, LLY above $960 kill-switch, GLD above $445 kill-switch). Qualitative refresh confirms: LLY Q4 EPS $7.40 (beat vs $7.35 est), KLAC FY2026-Q2 EPS $8.73 with forward acceleration to $11.53 by Q4 2026, JPM Q4 EPS $4.64 solid with Q1-Q3 2026 estimates $5.41-$5.44, XOM Q4 EPS $1.54 with 2026 estimates $1.60-$1.83. All four quality anchors intact. Portfolio holds. Next rebalance window: March 4 at earliest.

thesis_damage_flags.why: 3m momentum deteriorated further to -14.72% (from -5.97% 3 weeks ago); 1m=-3.33%; dd63=-25.26%; below MA50. Composite mom=+12.64% only maintained by 12m=+60.27% tail. TSM now clearly dominant semiconductor peer with mom=+51.14%, 3m=+32.74%, 1m=+11.37%, dd63=-10.46%. Swap candidacy is elevated — decision point at March 4 rebalance.

Short-term momentum soft: 1m=-1.52%, 3m=-4.34%, below MA50 ($1051.85). However, Q4 2025 EPS $7.40 beat consensus, 2026 EPS ramp ($7.35→$8.21→$9.18→$9.47) intact, 12m=+14.17% and above MA200. Thesis NOT broken — monitoring, not acting. Kill-switch at $960.

thesis: Non-rebalance day discipline enforced: Day 1 post Feb-26 run; 7-day minimum cadence places next eligible rebalance window at March 4 at earliest. No emergency override conditions are present — all kill-switch levels intact (AVGO >$300 ✓ at $321.70, LLY >$960 ✓ at $1022.02, GLD >$445 ✓ at $477.48).

Qualitative refresh validates current holdings: LLY Q4 EPS $7.40 vs $7.35 est (beat), 2026 quarterly ramp confirmed ($7.35→$8.21→$9.18→$9.47), quality compounders thesis intact at 83% gross margin / high ROE. KLAC FY2026-Q2 EPS $8.73 with acceleration to $9.10→$9.74→$10.62→$11.53 over next 4 quarters — process control dominance unbroken. XOM Q4 EPS $1.54 with 2026 estimates $1.60-$1.83 providing solid value anchor. JPM Q4 EPS $4.64 with 2026 estimates $5.33-$5.44 per quarter, P/E discipline intact.

March 4 rebalance pre-positioning notes: (1) AVGO→TSM swap is now highest-conviction candidate (TSM: mom=+51.14%, 3m=+32.74%, 1m=+11.37%, dd63=-10.46% vs AVGO: 3m=-14.72%, dd63=-25.26%); (2) AVGO→LMT alternative (LMT: mom=+35.36%, 3m=+43.33%, dd63=-5.23%) offers lower-vol defense sector with clean trend; (3) LLY weight reduction from 12% to 8-10% with reallocation to LMT or TSM is a candidate action if 3m momentum deterioration continues; (4) GLD 18% allocation is validated by sustained gold rally (mom=+39.16%) — no change warranted.
risks: MACRO: SPY -0.89% 1m and QQQ -3.47% 1m with QQQ below MA50 signals fragile risk appetite. A tariff escalation shock or hawkish Fed pivot could compress energy multiples (XOM+CVX=27% combined) and financials (JPM 12%) simultaneously — 39% combined cyclical exposure is the primary macro vulnerability. TLT +3.21% 1m bond bid could reverse sharply on strong economic data, hurting gold's safe-haven premium.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: AVGO 3m=-14.72% continues deteriorating — further leg down risks breaking composite momentum thesis entirely before March 4 window. LLY below MA50 with competitive GLP-1 dynamics from pipeline rivals could accelerate 3m drawdown beyond -4.34% current. KLAC 1m=-5.57% (though 3m=+34.27% remains strong) could see continued near-term semiconductor inventory cycle headwinds.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: SLV (vol20=153.5%, dd63=-37.15%, 1m=-20.81%) remains a dangerous add despite mom=+90.12% — extreme volatility and drawdown risk would spike portfolio vol materially. GLD vol20=55.44% at 18% weight already contributes meaningful volatility. If March 4 rebalance involves 3 simultaneous changes, staging over 2 sessions is warranted given energy positions at 27% combined weight.
falsifiable_checks: If AVGO closes below $300 before March 4, initiate emergency review and replace with TSM (close=$376.81, mom=+51.14%, dd63=-10.46%) at same weight.
If LLY closes below $960 (approx -6% from $1022.02, near MA200=$870 support zone entry) before March 4, reduce from 12% to 8% and flag LMT (mom=+35.36%) as partial replacement candidate.
If GLD closes below $445 (approx -6.8% from $477.48) before March 4, reduce 18% GLD allocation to 12-14% — parabolic extension with vol20=55.44% makes asymmetric drawdown risk real on macro surprise.
If TSM 1m momentum remains above +10% by March 4, score AVGO→TSM swap as a confirmed rebalance action with 10% weight transfer (total semiconductor weight unchanged at 10%).
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Day 1 of 7-day minimum hold cadence. Base case: energy/gold leadership extends into March, KLAC re-rates on earnings acceleration ($8.73 actual → $11.53 est by Q4 2026), JPM and XOM provide quality/value anchors. Bull case: crude oil recovers, GLD extends toward $500 on global uncertainty, KLAC and XOM add to sector alpha. Bear case: tariff shock + tech mean-reversion creates correlation spike hitting energy/financials simultaneously; 39% cyclical concentration is the primary stress point. Process discipline preserved — no edge clears transaction cost hurdle today. Next scheduled decision: March 4, 2026.

constraints_check.notes: FACTOR SCORES (0-100): XOM=74 (mom=28.38%, 3m=28.94%, dd63=-5.48%, quality anchor, EPS est $1.60-$1.83), CVX=70 (mom=19.18%, 3m=24.18%, low dd63=-5.28%, clean trend), GLD=76 (mom=39.16%, 3m=25.59%, dominant safe-haven, above all MAs), KLAC=78 (mom=53.17%, 3m=34.27%, EPS $8.73→$11.53 ramp, quality=43% op margin), XLI=72 (mom=18.56%, dd63=-2.66%, lowest volatility holding vol20=14.64%), JPM=65 (mom=7.50%, quality financials, EPS $5.41-$5.44 est, below MA50 penalty), LLY=60 (mom=11.90%, Q4 beat, 2026 ramp intact, but 3m=-4.34% and below MA50 — reduced conviction), AVGO=48 (mom=12.64% composite but 3m=-14.72%, dd63=-25.26%, below MA50 — elevated replace risk). | RISK BUDGET: Energy=27% (XOM+CVX), Tech=20% (KLAC+AVGO), Commodities=18% (GLD), Healthcare=12% (LLY), Financials=12% (JPM), Industrials=11% (XLI). Max single position=18% (GLD, within 40% cap). Max sector=27% (Energy, well within 80% cap). Crypto=0% (within 10% cap). Total=100%. | SCENARIO STRESS: Equity index -10%: energy/financials hit (-39% cyclical), GLD offsets as safe-haven, net portfolio drawdown estimated -5 to -7%. Rates shock +50bps: GLD -3%, JPM neutral-positive, energy neutral, net modest negative. Commodity shock -20%: energy -27% x 20% = -5.4% portfolio drag, partially offset by GLD. USD +5% shock: GLD -2%, XOM/CVX international ops mildly pressured, net -2 to -3% portfolio. | EXECUTION PLAN: Non-rebalance day — no trades. March 4 pre-staging: evaluate AVGO→TSM at 10% weight (no sector weight change) as primary action. Secondary: evaluate LLY 12%→10% reallocation to LMT (2%) if 3m momentum remains negative. Maximum 3 changes at March 4 rebalance. | MONITORING/KILL-SWITCHES: AVGO<$300 → emergency replace with TSM. LLY<$960 → reduce to 8%, flag LMT add. GLD<$445 → reduce from 18% to 12-14%. KLAC below MA50 ($1407) on weekly close → reassess tech sleeve sizing. QQQ 1m below -8% sustained → increase GLD to 20%, reduce AVGO to 6%.
